counterfactual_scenarios.title: What if Bumrah's 17th over was average?
counterfactual_scenarios.change: Jasprit Bumrah's over 17 conceded 10 runs. If it had yielded average (8.25 economy = ~5 runs), India wins by 12 runs instead of 7.
counterfactual_scenarios.lola_outcome: India would have won by 12 runs
counterfactual_scenarios.narrative: Bumrah's death bowling was clinical overall, but his 17th over against Jacob Bethell leaked runs. In this scenario, his economy stays at his tournament average. The psychological impact would have been immense — England's biggest dangerman would have faced a 246-run equation instead of 241, significantly compressing their margin for error in the final overs. This hypothetical emphasizes how India's bowlers held their nerve when it mattered most.

counterfactual_scenarios.title: What if Bethell faced Varun from over 8?
counterfactual_scenarios.change: Jacob Bethell scored 105(48) but struggled early against Varun Chakravarthy. If Bethell's SR vs Varun matched his overall SR, his matchup vs Varun yields 5 fewer runs.
counterfactual_scenarios.lola_outcome: England still would have fallen 12 runs short
counterfactual_scenarios.narrative: Bethell's brilliance was undeniable, but there were tactical moments where India's spinners had the measure of him. In this counterfactual, Bethell's conversion rate against Varun reflects his overall form instead of a brief power-play anomaly. Even with this adjustment, England's case remains dire — they'd still need miracles in the final overs. It highlights that singular batting partnerships, even elite ones, cannot salvage a chase unless the entire team contributes.

game_changers.impact: Player of Match. 89(42) with 8 fours, 7 sixes. His explosive batting provided the backbone India needed.
Valiant counter-attack: 105(48) with 8 fours, 7 sixes. Nearly took England home single-handedly.
4 overs, 1 wicket, 33 runs. Exceptional economy (8.25) and containment in death.
